description.value:
Humans

Stereotype: Quick to build, quick to die, quick to break their word.

Size: 1M; Speed: 5; Stability: 0

an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.name: Purchased Traits
an4b9HdNrni1iSlM.description: You have 3 ancestry points to spend on the following traits.

description.value: As a maneuver, you can open your awareness to detect supernatural creatures and phenomena. Until the end of your next turn, you know the location of any supernatural object, or any undead, construct, or creature from another world within 5 squares, even if you don't have line of effect. You know if you're detecting an item or a creature, and its nature.
Ignore temporary difficult terrain created by magic/psionics. Reduce forced movement from such sources by 1.
Edge on Endurance tests. Your *slowed* speed is 3 instead of 2.
When you take the Help action, the bonus granted is +2 instead of +1.

A chartered frontier company that works the ash-woods and burned margins: disciplined patrols, quotas, and the grim etiquette of boundary law.

Language: AskeskogsmÃ¥l

Environment: Wilderness; Organization: Bureaucratic; Upbringing: Martial

Skills: History, Insight, Streetwise.

Languages: One additional language.

Renown: 1; Wealth: 0; Project Points: 150

Perk: *Paper Shield*. Once per session, you can produce documentation (real or plausible) that grants an edge on one social test made in a lawful setting.
Whenever you make a test that uses the Brag, Flirt, or Intimidate skills, you can use Might instead of any other characteristic the test calls for.

Strategist. Defender. Leader. With weapon in hand, you lead allies into the maw of battle, barking out commands that inspire your fellow heroes to move faster and strike more precisely. All the while, you stand between your compatriots and death, taunting the followers of evil to best you if they can.

As a tactician, you have abilities that heal your allies and grant them increased damage, movement, and attacks, even as you leave your enemies struggling to respond.

KoGp0Q93aEXIiQxx.name: Out of Position
KoGp0Q93aEXIiQxx.description: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics.

lffZjjuNWAvfmliT.name: Skills
lffZjjuNWAvfmliT.description: Choose any two skills from Alertness, Architecture, Blacksmithing, Brag, Culture, Empathize, Fletching, Mechanics, Monsters, Search, Strategy, or the skills of the exploration skill group. (Quick Build: Monsters, Strategy.)

TPNKDjRP9rjsZfdo.name: Tactical Doctrine
TPNKDjRP9rjsZfdo.description:
Warfare is as old as civilizationâ€”and perhaps even older. As battle became ever more complex, military leaders invented tactical doctrine, outlining how combatants should be structured, used, and deployed. Doctrine can be learned at war colleges passing on ancient martial traditions, or directly through blood and sweat on the battlefield. Whatever path brought you to your mastery of historically proven tactics, you choose a tactical doctrine from the following options, each of which grants you a skill. (Quick Build: Vanguard and the Intimidate skill.)

Your tactical doctrine is your subclass, and your choice of doctrine determines many of the features youâ€™ll gain as you gain new levels.

QbsiJwC2zZCfmQej.name: Focus
QbsiJwC2zZCfmQej.description: The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

7k50JsLsnWjsjUNC.name: Field Arsenal
7k50JsLsnWjsjUNC.description: You have drilled with a broad array of weapons and have developed techniques to optimize their use. Whenever you select or change your kit, you can select an additional kit and gain the benefits of both kits, including both their signature abilities. See Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper)

MP2iZkK0a0TBrk2f.description: You know how to focus the attention of your allies as you push them toward victory.

description.value:
You have drilled with a broad array of arms and armor, and have developed techniques to optimize their use. You can use and gain the benefits of two kits, including both their signature abilities. Whenever you would choose or change one kit, you can choose or change your second kit as well. See Chapter 6: Kits for more information. (Quick Build: Shining Armor, Sniper.)

If both kits grant you the same benefit, you take one or the other and canâ€™t change your choice until you finish a respite. (This usually means taking the higher of two bonuses.)

For example, if you take the Shining Armor and Sniper kits, you gain the following benefits overall:

  • Stamina Bonus: +12 per echelon

  • Stability Bonus: +1

  • Melee Damage Bonus: +2/+2/+2

  • Ranged Damage Bonus: +0/+0/+4

  • Speed Bonus: +1

  • Ranged Distance Bonus: +10

  • Disengage Bonus: +1

  • You can use the Patient Shot and Protective Attack signature abilities.

Kit signature abilities have their kitâ€™s bonuses already applied, which might require you to adjust the bonuses of the signature abilities you gain from a kit. For example, you might take the Martial Artist kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +2/+2/+2, and the Mountain kit, which gives a melee weapon damage bonus of +0/+0/+4. If you choose to use the Mountain kitâ€™s damage bonus, then the Battle Grace signature ability from the Martial Artist kit loses the +2/+2/+2 bonus from that kit, reducing its usual 5/8/11 damage for its tier 1, tier 2, and tier 3 outcomes to 3/6/9. It then gains the +0/+0/+4 of the Mountain kit to deal 3/6/13 damage.

The retiarius is often depicted as a lightly armored warrior with a net in one hand and a trident in the other, and this kit gives you the equipment and fighting technique to make that happen. Tie up your foe with a net and then poke them to death!

system.story: The well-thrown net that follows your main attack leaves your foes right where you want them

description.value: The Whirlwind kit makes effective use of whips, granting you mobility, damage, and reach. If you want to be a fast-moving warrior who lashes foes with a chain or whip, then this is the kit for you

system.story: When you draw your whip back after an attack, your enemy is drawn ever closer

description.value:
The ring of steel panics others but brings order to your mind, granting you a Heroic Resource called focus.

Focus in Combat

At the start of a combat encounter or some other stressful situation tracked in combat rounds (as determined by the Director), you gain focus equal to your Victories. At the start of each of your turns during combat, you gain 2 focus.

Additionally, the first time each combat round that you or any ally damages a creature marked by you (see @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.zyd6C7phOZr6EAQ9]{Mark}), you gain 1 focus. The first time in a combat round that any ally within 10 squares of you uses a heroic ability, you gain 1 focus.

You lose any remaining focus at the end of the encounter.

Focus Outside of Combat

Though you canâ€™t gain focus outside of combat, you can use your heroic abilities and effects that cost focus without spending it. Whenever you use an ability or effect outside of combat that costs focus, you canâ€™t use that same ability or effect outside of combat again until you earn 1 or more Victories or finish a respite.

When you use an ability outside of combat that lets you spend unlimited focus on its effect, you can use it as if you had spent an amount of focus equal to your Victories.

system.story: You draw your alliesâ€™ attention to a specific foeâ€”with devastating effect.

effect.before:
The target is [[/apply YW2JEdoDPd0XlaII]]{marked} by you until the end of the encounter, until you are dying, or until you use this ability again. You can willingly end your mark on a creature (no action required), and if another tactician marks a creature, your mark on that creature ends. When a creature marked by you is reduced to 0 Stamina, you can use a free triggered action to mark a new target within distance.

You can initially mark only one creature using this ability, though other tactician abilities allow you to mark additional creatures at the same time. The mastermind tactical doctrineâ€™s Anticipation feature allows you to target additional creatures with this ability starting at 5th level.

While a creature marked by you is within your line of effect, you and allies within your line of effect gain an edge on power rolls made against that creature. Additionally, whenever you or any ally uses an ability to deal rolled damage to a creature marked by you, you can spend 1 focus to gain one of the following benefits as a free triggered action:

  • The ability deals extra [[/damage 2*@R]]{damage} equal to twice your Reason score.

  • The creature dealing the damage can spend a Recovery.

  • The creature dealing the damage can shift up to a number of squares equal to [[lookup @R]]{your Reason score}.

  • If you damage a creature marked by you with a melee ability, the creature is [[/apply taunted turn]] by you until the end of their next turn.

You canâ€™t gain more than one benefit from the same trigger

description.value: Even before battle begins, your enemies struggle to keep up with your tactics. At the start of an encounter, you can use a free triggered action to use your Mark ability against one enemy you have line of effect to, even if you are surprised. You can then slide the marked target up to 3 squares, ignoring stability. The target canâ€™t be moved in a way that would harm them (such as over a cliff), leave them dying, or result in them suffering a condition or other negative effect.
You can don or remove a disguise as part of any test you make using the Hide skill, or while using the Hide maneuver.

system.story: Your foe left an opening. You point this out to an ally!

effect.before: The target can use a signature ability as a free triggered action.

spend.text: You target two allies instead of one.

description.value: You have learned the stratagems of ancient heroes, letting you lead from the front lines and seek victory through sheer force of will and personality. You gain a skill from the interpersonal skill group.

GK5Xal3qdQKHwNvz.description: Your tactical doctrine grants you the following feature.

50Ss8Xh8svc2ppfs.name: 9th-Level Vanguard Ability
50Ss8Xh8svc2ppfs.description: Your tactical doctrine grants your choice of one of two heroic abilities.

description.value: You command any room you walk into. While you are present during a negotiation, each hero with you treats their Renown as 2 higher than usual. Additionally, each hero with you during a combat encounter has a double edge on tests made to stop combat and start a negotiation.

After constant drills, you can more accurately anticipate an enemyâ€™s plan and thwart their attempts to move across the battlefield. Whenever you make an opportunity attack, the targetâ€™s speed is reduced to 0 until the end of the current turn.

Mark Benefit: When a creature marked by you attempts to move or shift within distance of your melee free strike, you can use a free triggered action and spend 2 focus to make a melee free strike against that creature

system.story: You prioritize saving an ally over your own safety.
system.trigger: The target deals damage to an ally.

tier1.display: {{potency}}, the target is frightened of the triggering ally (save ends)

effect.before: You move up to your speed toward the triggering ally, ending this movement adjacent to them or in the nearest square if you canâ€™t reach an adjacent square. The triggering ally can spend a Recovery and gains [[/heal 5 temporary]] for each enemy you came adjacent to during the move. You then make a power roll against the target.

system.story: Your quick reflexes cost an enemy the precision they seek.
system.trigger: A creature deals damage to the target.

effect.before: You can shift 1 square. If the target is you, or if you end this shift adjacent to the target, the target takes half the damage. If the damage has any potency effect associated with it, the potency is decreased by 1.

spend.text: This abilityâ€™s distance becomes Melee 1 + your  Reason score, and you can shift up to a number of squares equal to  your Reason score instead of 1 square.

system.story: On your command, you and your allies force back the enemy line.

effect.before: Each target can move up to their speed.

system.story: â€œLetâ€™s not argue about whoâ€™s the hammer and whoâ€™s the anvil!

tier1.display: one ally within 10 squares can make a signature strike against the target as a free triggered action

tier2.display: one ally within 10 squares can make a signature strike that gains an edge against the target as a free triggered action

tier3.display: two allies within 10 squares can each make a signature strike that gains an edge against the target as free triggered actions

effect.after: If the target is reduced to 0 Stamina and a strike granted by this ability hasnâ€™t been made, the striker can pick a different target.

effect.before: Until the end of the encounter or until you are dying, whenever you or any ally deals damage to a target marked by you who has R < [Average], the target is [[/apply frightened save]] of the creature who dealt the damage.
Choose an enemy adjacent to you. The next ability power roll an ally who makes against that creature before the start of your next turn has an edge.

A creature who uses the Catch Breath maneuver spends a Recovery and regains [[/heal @recoveries.recoveryValue]]{Stamina} equal to their recovery value.

A creature who is dying canâ€™t use the Catch Breath maneuver, but other creatures can help them spend Recoveries in other ways.

When a creature takes the Charge main action, they move up to their speed in a straight line without jumping, then make a @UUID[Compendium.draw-steel.abilities.Item.wU69Y06G9lYFrvp6]{melee free strike} against a target when they end their move. If the creature has an ability with the Charge keyword, they can use that ability against the target instead of a free strike.

A creature canâ€™t move through difficult terrain or shift when they charge. They can fly or burrow as part of the Charge main action if they have that movement available to them, but they canâ€™t climb or swim while charging unless they can automatically use that movement at full speed.

effects.name: Defending
effects.description: When a creature takes the Defend main action, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.

effect.before: When a creature takes the [[/apply TYggdI2Kwa302NjW]]{Defend main action}, ability rolls made against them have a double bane until the start of their next turn. Additionally, you have a double edge on tests when called for to resist environmental effects or a creatureâ€™s traits or abilities. A creature gains no benefit from this action while another creature is taunted by them.
A creature who is grabbed by another creature, an object, or an effect (see Grab below) can attempt to escape by using the following ability.
effect.after: You take a bane on this maneuver if your size is smaller than the size of the creature, object, or effect that has you grabbed.

tier2.display: You can grab the target, but if you do, the target can make a melee free strike against you before they are grabbed

tier3.display: The target is grabbed by you.

effect.before: A creature seeking to keep a foe close and locked down can attempt to grab a creature using the following ability
effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature who uses the Heal main action employs medicine or inspiring words to make an adjacent creature feel better and stay in the fight. The target creature can spend a Recovery to regain Stamina, or can make a saving throw against one effect they are suffering that is ended by a saving throw.

effect.after: You can usually target only creatures of your size or smaller. If your Might score is 2 or higher, you can target any creature with a size equal to or less than your Might score.

effect.before: A creature can use the Stand Up maneuver to stand up if they are prone, ending that condition. Alternatively, they can use this maneuver to make a willing adjacent prone creature stand up.
When a creature takes the Advance move action, they move a number of squares up to their speed. They can break up this movement with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

When a creature takes the Disengage move action, they can shift [[lookup @movement.disengage]]{1} square(s). Certain class features, kits, and other rules allow a creature to shift more than 1 square when they disengage. A creature who does so can break up their shift with their maneuver and main action however they wish.

A creature can take the Ride move action only while mounted on another creature. When a creature takes the Ride move action, they cause their mount to move up to the mountâ€™s speed, taking the rider with them. Alternatively, a creature can use the Ride move action to have their mount use the Disengage move action as a free triggered action. A creature can use the Ride move action only once per round. A mounted creature can only have this move action applied to them once per round. This movement can be broken up with the riderâ€™s maneuver and main action however they wish.

biography.value:
Brynja SteinsdÃ³ttir is a broad-shouldered woman with calloused hands and the steady voice of someone who has been making decisions for forty people and hasn't slept in days. Before the Burning worsened, she was a trade-caravan warden â€” her instinct for logistics kept the refugee column alive.

She leads approximately forty people â€” families, children, elderly, a few traders â€” southwest toward Hrafnborg. She organized them into a column when the situation deteriorated and has been the practical center of their survival since.

She watches the party carefully during any exchange. She is the one who will actually decide whether the refugees trust these strangers.
biography.director:
BEAT 23 â€” THE ASHEN ROAD / WAYSTATION SETTLEMENT

She/her. Former trade-caravan warden. Practical, unflappable, exhausted. She has been making life-or-death decisions for forty people for two weeks and her patience for empty reassurance is zero.

ROLE: Light negotiation target. She decides whether the refugees trust the party. If the party earns her trust, the refugees may travel with them â€” adding 40 people to the group moving toward Hrafnborg.

DEMEANOR:

  • Interest rises: Her shoulders relax slightly. She nods â€” not warm, but acknowledging. "You've been honest. That's more than most."
  • Interest flat: Watching, arms folded. "Tell me something I don't know."
  • Interest drops: Her jaw tightens. She steps half a pace back from the party. "We've heard enough promises."

REFUGEE CONSEQUENCE: If refugees travel with the party, they slow movement by one day (tightens Beat 24 deadline). They are present at Hrafnborg â€” more mouths, more stakes, but also future seeds of recovery. In the siege (Beat 25), they add to the emotional weight of defense.

VIGMUND'S OATH NOTE: If the party swore the Advocacy Oath in Beat 13, these refugees are the living embodiment of that promise â€” 40 more people depending on the party's word at Hrafnborg's council table. If they also swore the Silence Oath (Beat 17), the conflict sharpens: they know about a warm refuge and cannot speak of it.
